title: audio-layer MFDR

project.name: audio-layer
project.description: AI product app managed by the AI Starter Kit.

hypothesis: If Unvalidated: define the narrow first customer before broad implementation. get a starter-enforced workflow for AI product app managed by the AI Starter Kit., they will trust agent-built software faster because every surface has plans, docs, tests, browser evidence, and cost visibility.
productThesis: AI product app managed by the AI Starter Kit.

decisions.choice: Validate or narrow before broad implementation
decisions.why: Unvalidated: define the narrow first customer before broad implementation.

decisions.choice: .ai-starter remains the source of truth for repo state
decisions.why: Plans, manifests, evidence, costs, docs, and scorecards survive chat compaction and runtime changes.
alternatives: Keep state only in chat transcripts
Use an external project tracker as the canonical source

decisions.choice: Track configured providers: assemblyai, custom-api-routes, resend, stripe, supabase, vercel-ai-gateway
decisions.why: External APIs need docs, env requirements, cost events, failure modes, and contract tests.
alternatives: Call providers directly without local registry

decisions.choice: Use DESIGN.md and token registry as the UI contract
decisions.why: Project-specific design system defined during setup; preserve existing product visual language when present.
alternatives: Let each agent invent visual direction
Require manual design review without local tokens
tradeoffs: Design drift can block strict projects
Visual consistency improves across agent sessions

decisions.choice: Require Playwright plus Expect browser-control proof for user-visible routes
decisions.why: Playwright gives deterministic browser checks; Expect records agent-style browser command evidence.
alternatives: No browser proof for fast iterations

apiAndServicePlan.label: AssemblyAI transcription
apiAndServicePlan.why: Use official docs and local registry entry before touching AssemblyAI transcription.
tests: Record dashboard evidence after a successful assemblyai run.

apiAndServicePlan.label: Custom API routes
apiAndServicePlan.why: Document Custom API routes behavior, pricing, failure modes, and test contract locally.
tests: Record dashboard evidence after a successful custom-api-routes run.

apiAndServicePlan.label: Resend email
apiAndServicePlan.why: Use official docs and local registry entry before touching Resend email.
tests: Record dashboard evidence after a successful resend run.

apiAndServicePlan.label: Stripe payments
apiAndServicePlan.why: Use official docs and local registry entry before touching Stripe payments.
tests: Record dashboard evidence after a successful stripe run.

apiAndServicePlan.label: Supabase
apiAndServicePlan.why: Use official docs and local registry entry before touching Supabase.
tests: Record dashboard evidence after a successful supabase run.

apiAndServicePlan.label: Vercel AI Gateway
apiAndServicePlan.why: Use official docs and local registry entry before touching Vercel AI Gateway.
tests: Record dashboard evidence after a successful vercel-ai-gateway run.

uiPlan.designContract: Project-specific design system defined during setup; preserve existing product visual language when present.
uiPlan.designTokens: Use DESIGN.md tokens and generated design registry before hardcoding visual values.
uiPlan.interactionModel: Clear task-first flows with visible feedback and recoverable states.
uiPlan.visualProof: Capture screenshots and Expect replays after visual route/component changes.

verificationPlan.browserProof: Run against the local dev server with AI_STARTER_BASE_URL or PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL set.

successMetrics: Product validation is complete or explicitly bypassed with a reason.
Feature plan has acceptance criteria and required evidence.
Expect proof reports successful browser-control commands for user-visible routes.
External APIs/services have cost events or an explicit provider-dashboard/manual tracking reason.
risks: The first customer/problem/workaround is not validated enough to justify broad build scope.
Pricing is not validated; support and compute cost may exceed willingness to pay.
Distribution is not validated; the first 100 users may be hard to reach.
MFDR can become stale if API/tool/UI decisions change without rerunning `pnpm mfdr`.
Browser proof requires a live local server and installed Expect CLI.
openQuestions: Complete product validation or record why it is bypassed.
nextStep: Before broad implementation, run `pnpm mfdr`, then `pnpm plan -- "<first feature>"`, then implement only the next verified slice.
